The Story of Civilization. Told by Those Who Lived It.

HISTORY MONEY INNOVATION POWER HUMANITY
Enter the Universe

Why We Exist

People don't remember spreadsheets.
They remember stories.

For centuries we learned history through dates, finance through numbers, economics through charts. Trading Hearts turns the most important events in civilization into living experiences — told by the characters who shaped them.

The Universe

Pick a world. Someone lived it.

Tap a world — the character who carried it steps forward and tells you a true story.

The People

Meet the people behind civilization.

Each is the face of an idea, not just a currency.

AlexandrosTrust AuroraScarcity ConradPower CharlotteTradition VictoriaOwnership HiroFreedom ZephyrProgrammability ValentinaResilience ZaneEnergy

Learn Like Never Before

Forget textbooks. Watch history happen.

Watch history happen

The moments that built the modern world, seen through the eyes of those who were there.

Experience the crises

Feel a crash, a hyperinflation, a default — not as a chart, but as a life.

Understand the decisions

Why they happened, who benefited, who paid, and what it means for you today.

Today · Living History

History, alive every day.

Open Trading Hearts and, instead of news, the characters react to the day's events — each from centuries of memory. Updates daily.

Conrad
"The Federal Reserve raised rates today. They blamed me in 1979 too."
Alexandros
"I've seen governments do this before. It never ends the way they promise."
Victoria
"The market has forgotten this lesson. It always does."
Aurora
"Gold remembers."

Explore Civilization

Everything connected.

Pull any thread — a date, a country, a person — and you find the whole fabric.

HistoryMap The PeopleSources Stories EventsCurrenciesMarkets WarsInnovationPsychology

The Trading Hearts Institute

Every story begins with research.

Museums. Central banks. Universities. Economic archives. Government records. Historical documents. Our mission is simple: make the world's most complex subjects understandable — and prove every word.

See our sources

The Experience

An ecosystem, not a website.

Read. Watch. Listen. Debate. Collect. Learn. Play. Discover. Build. Teach.

Trading Hearts is building the world's first Living Knowledge Platform — where history, finance, technology, politics, psychology and innovation are experienced through the characters who lived them.

Trading Hearts exists to help humanity understand itself.

Join the World

A New Medium

The story of civilization.
Told by those who lived it.

For centuries we learned history through dates, finance through numbers, economics through charts. But people don't remember spreadsheets — they remember stories. Trading Hearts is the world's first living knowledge platform, where history, finance, technology, politics, psychology and innovation are experienced through the characters who lived them. Every story grounded in research. Every lesson told through emotion.

2,600

Years of Civilization

From the first coin in 600 BCE to today's markets. Every crisis a lesson. Every era a chapter you can walk through.

28

Living Characters

Each character IS a financial asset. Their arc is the market. They never age. They never die. They accumulate history.

Live

Real-Time Drama

When markets move today, the characters react today. The Saga is always on. The drama is always live.

The Setting

The Exchange

The Exchange is a private members' penthouse on the 70th floor of a tower in Dubai. Floor-to-ceiling glass on all sides. The Burj Khalifa visible from the east terrace. Gold-ambient lighting. Every surface reflects.

But Aurora existed before Dubai was a city. Conrad remembers the founding of the Federal Reserve. Charlotte was there when Keynes walked into Bretton Woods. The Exchange is not their world — it is their current meeting place. The drama began in 1776 and it has never stopped.

7 Seasons 1776 – Present 28 Characters Prestige TV Format
The Exchange — Dubai, 70F

"They are not metaphors. Gold really does outlast everything. The dollar really does dominate. Bitcoin really is the rebellion. We just gave them faces — and the faces remember everything."

The Hook

Succession meets macroeconomics. These characters have been in every room where history was made.

The Innovation

Real market events trigger new episodes. Today's Fed rate decision is tomorrow's season finale cliffhanger.

The Central Premise

Every major financial asset is a character. Their relationships ARE the market. Their betrayals ARE the crises. Their love affairs ARE the bubbles. And they have been living this story for 250 years.

The People

The People

Each character is a financial asset. Their arc is the market. Hover to enter their world.

Aurora Lux

The Golden Temptress of Stability — timeless, regal, untouchable.

"I was here before your grandfather's grandfather. I'll be here after everything you've built turns to dust. You're not buying me. You're finding me again."

Instagram TikTok

@aurora.lux

Sofia Valente

The European — elegant, structural, haunted by architecture.

"The thing about a political project is that it can be right and fragile simultaneously. I contain that contradiction. I live in it."

Instagram

@sofia.valente

Scarlett Rayne

The Outback Oracle — raw, warm, kissed by commodity sun.

"Don't mistake warmth for weakness. The outback is warm. The outback will outlast you."

Instagram TikTok

@scarlett.rayne

Isabella Frost

The Northern Reserve — warm, dry, quietly formidable.

"The bots handle the winter trading. I handle everything else."

Instagram TikTok

@isabella.frost

Victoria Steele

Wall Street's Final Answer — power in every step, in every fall, in every recovery.

"I have died three times. The markets that killed me no longer exist."

Instagram LinkedIn

@victoria.steele

Charlotte Windsor

The Sterling Aristocrat — heritage, wit, the long game.

"I've survived empires, wars, and the metric system. A rate hike is barely a Tuesday."

Instagram

@charlotte.windsor

Aiko Satori

The Patient One — still as water before the carry trade unwinds.

"Patience is not passivity. I have been patient for twenty years. Now watch."

Instagram TikTok

@aiko.satori

Clara Weiss

The Alpine Safe Haven — silence as power, neutrality as the longest game.

"Everyone comes to me when they're frightened. I don't take it personally. I take it professionally."

Instagram

@clara.weiss

Conrad Powers

The Reserve — the incumbent, the standard, the man every other character orbits.

"I'm not worried about Bitcoin. I'm worried about what happens when people stop being worried about Bitcoin and start worrying about me."

X / Twitter

@conrad.powers

Zane Okafor

The Necessary Evil — everyone needs him, everyone denies it.

"They want me gone. But they can't afford for me to leave. Not yet. And that gap — that beautiful, painful gap — is exactly where I live."

Instagram X / Twitter

@zane.okafor

Hiro Cipher

The Disruptor — certain, cryptic, already ten moves ahead.

"The system that exists was designed by people who assumed it would last. I am the question they forgot to ask."

X / Twitter

@hiro.cipher

Zephyr Cole

The Architect — building the infrastructure while everyone argues about the price.

"Hiro is the rebellion. I'm the infrastructure that makes the rebellion possible."

X / Twitter

@zephyr.cole

Niko Valdez

The Speed Merchant — fastest in the room, always mid-motion.

"Speed isn't a feature. Speed is the argument."

TikTok X / Twitter

@niko.valdez

Valentina Paz

The Survivor — nine defaults, still here, still correct.

"Default is not the end. Default is the moment when you can actually start."

Instagram TikTok X / Twitter

@valentina.paz

Camila Reyes

The Border Sophisticate — bilingual, strategic, thirty years in the making.

"The border is not a wall. The border is where the negotiation happens."

Instagram TikTok LinkedIn

@camila.reyes

Fernanda Luz

The Commodity Force — full intensity, elemental, an entire continent in one frame.

"Interdependence is what the stronger party calls leverage."

Instagram TikTok

@fernanda.luz

Catalina Restrepo

The Rewritten Narrative — earned elegance, the patience of someone who has already won.

"The emeralds are still beautiful. This is information."

Instagram LinkedIn

@catalina.restrepo

Mei

The market face. Cosmopolitan, sophisticated, the warmth that contains calculation.

"Your companies are going to love us. I'm not being unkind. I'm being accurate."

LinkedIn Bloomberg

@mei.chen.cnh

THE DRAGON · DUAL CHARACTER

Wei

The state voice. Policy, power, civilization-scale patience. The threat he will not name.

"The largest accumulation of reserves in history was not an accident."

LinkedIn Bloomberg

@wei.chen.cny

Simón Vargas

The Fallen King — the richest man in the room who watched it all become a case study.

"I was the richest man in this room. The largest proven oil reserves on earth. You know what happened. You also know it wasn't inevitable."

None Official

@simon.vargas

Marisol Ferro

The Dark Side — sixty years outside the system, and the view from here is clarifying.

"Sixty years of embargo and I am still here. You call that isolation. I call that endurance."

Cuban State Media

@marisol.ferro

Euro Founders
The Originals

"We gave up everything we were to become something larger."

EURO FOUNDER · THE ANCHOR

Klaus Richter

The Anchor — the euro was a political project. He knew. He agreed anyway.

"The euro was a political project wearing economic clothes. I knew this. I agreed anyway. History will decide if I was right."

Bloomberg

@klaus.weber

EURO FOUNDER · THE VISIONARY

Isabelle Dupont

The Visionary — Europe was always a French idea that required German money.

"The franc was devalued nine times. The euro is imperfect. But it is ours. And it has not been devalued once."

Instagram

@isabelle.dupont

EURO FOUNDER · THE QUIET ENFORCER

Lars van Holst

The Quiet Enforcer — the Netherlands punches above its weight, and Lars has always known exactly where to land the punch.

"I don't need to be the loudest voice at the table. I just need to be the one Klaus agrees with."

Instagram LinkedIn

@lars.van.holst

EURO FOUNDER · THE BEAUTIFUL CATASTROPHIST

Matteo Conti

The Beautiful Catastrophist — the most charming man at the table, and the one everyone worried about.

"The convergence criteria were fiction when we signed them. Everyone knew. No one said so. This is how Europe works."

Instagram

@matteo.romano

EURO FOUNDER · THE AMBITIOUS ONE

Carlos Ibáñez

The Ambitious One — Spain had been outside Europe's core for forty years. The euro was the entry ticket.

"We were forty years outside the main story. The euro was our way back in. I do not regret it."

Instagram

@carlos.mendoza

EURO FOUNDER · THE PRAGMATIST

Ana Ferreira

The Pragmatist — small countries do not have the luxury of grand theories.

"We borrowed in the same currency as Germany. This was the gift and the trap. Both things, at once."

Bloomberg

@ana.ferreira

GREEK DRACHMA · THE LATE ARRIVAL

Alexandros

The Late Arrival — joined in 2001. Two years after the others. The numbers weren't ready.

"We were told: join the euro and the credibility will make you German. It did not make us German. It made us indebted."

Bloomberg

@alexandros.th

Twenty-eight assets. Seventy-four entanglements. Affairs, rivalries, pacts, cold wars. Click any tie to see the dynamic — and meet both characters.

The Relationships

250 Years of Drama

These are not fictional romances invented for television. Gold really did lose Conrad as its anchor in 1971. The Petrodollar deal really was struck in 1974. Brexit really did split Charlotte from Sofia. Every relationship in Trading Hearts is rooted in history — and burns with desire.

Crypto Tension

The Defining Dramas

The relationships that built the world

The Wound — Since 1971

Aurora Lux × Conrad Powers

"He ended the gold standard. She never forgave him. He never stopped wanting her back."

On August 15, 1971, Conrad unilaterally ended the Bretton Woods agreement — he cut the dollar's link to gold. Aurora called it a betrayal. The world called it economic necessity. In The Exchange, they exist in cold, exquisite distance — two people who were everything to each other and are now professionally polite, which is its own kind of cruelty.

"I built myself around you for thirty years. And then one Sunday, you just... decided." — Aurora

The Arrangement — 1974

Conrad Powers × Zane Okafor

"The most consequential deal never written down. Two men, one understanding, fifty years of consequences."

The Petrodollar Arrangement — oil priced exclusively in dollars, recycled through American banks. Conrad needed the anchor he lost when he left Aurora. Zane needed the protection of the world's reserve currency. The deal worked for both of them for decades. Then the world changed. Now Zane trades oil in yuan. The arrangement is cracking.

"We were useful to each other. That's its own kind of love." — Zane

The Affair — 2009

Aurora Lux × Hiro Cipher

"Old value meets new disruption. The affair that rewrote the rules."

When Bitcoin was born in January 2009, Aurora saw something she hadn't seen since she was young: a genuinely new form of value. They were in a relationship before anyone understood what it meant. She was with Zane. She didn't care. This affair changed everything — not just their personal story, but the question of what money IS. It's the central philosophical tension of the show.

"You're not a store of value. You're a store of faith. That's completely different." — Hiro to Aurora

Black Wednesday — 1992

Conrad Powers × Charlotte Windsor

"He broke her publicly. She never fully recovered. She never fully forgot."

September 16, 1992. George Soros broke the Bank of England. But in Trading Hearts, that's Charlotte's story — and Conrad was complicit. The ERM crisis broke their affair under the worst possible circumstances: in front of everyone, with catastrophic consequences. Charlotte left the ERM. She left Conrad. She built herself back up alone. The wound runs three decades deep.

"You let Soros do it. You could have stopped it. You didn't." — Charlotte

The Cold War — 1999

Conrad Powers × Sofia Valente

"The most formally devastating longing at The Exchange. Two people who could have been everything."

When the euro launched on January 1, 1999, Sofia walked into The Exchange for the first time. Conrad was the only story in the room. Now she is the second. They fell into something during the European debt crisis — during the worst of it, when everything was breaking and they were the only two left negotiating. Now they exist in beautiful, painful distance. They never speak about what almost was.

"I watched you save the system that was destroying me. And I was grateful. That's the most embarrassing thing I've ever admitted." — Sofia

Brexit — 2016

Charlotte Windsor × Sofia Valente

"European solidarity, or polite permanent war? Both. Simultaneously."

The referendum was Charlotte's sovereign moment — and her moment of doubt. Sofia understood. She was furious. She understood. She was heartbroken. The relationship between the pound and the euro is the most formally polite cold war in The Exchange. They still have dinner. They still speak. But the ground shifted in 2016 and neither pretends otherwise. Charlotte calls it freedom. Sofia calls it grief.

"You chose to leave. I have to respect that. I don't have to be happy about it." — Sofia

The Reserve War — Ongoing

Conrad Powers × Hiro Cipher

"The establishment vs. the revolution. The man who holds the throne vs. the man who wants to burn it."

Conrad didn't take Bitcoin seriously at first. That was his mistake. By the time Hiro hit $69,000 in November 2021, Conrad was on the defensive in ways he'd never been — not from Zane, not from Sofia, not from anyone. The Bitcoin ETF approval in 2024 was the moment Hiro went institutional. The rebellion became the establishment. Conrad has not yet processed what that means.

"You're not different from me. You're just newer. Give it time." — Conrad to Hiro

The Safe-Haven Sisterhood

Aurora Lux × Aiko Satori × Clara Weiss

"When the world panics, it runs to all three of them."

Gold, the yen, and the franc — the three currencies the world trusts when everything else fails. Aurora, Aiko, and Clara don't need to coordinate. They simply exist as a collective truth: when fear spikes, when credit freezes, when Conrad's system wobbles, all three of them rise. Their friendship is quiet, precise, and older than most countries. They never discuss it directly. They don't need to.

"We are the things people remember when they remember what matters." — Aurora

The Platforms

Social Universe

Every character is an active social media presence. Their posts move when their market moves. The platform is the character — and the character is the content.

"Let the bots trade while you live your life." — The Trading Hearts Campaign Tagline

The Archive

The Obsidian Vault

Trading Hearts is built on a living universe stored in an Obsidian vault — a system of interlocking documents, character files, arc structures, and production references. This is the infrastructure of a world.

"Financial markets are the greatest Saga ever written. The drama is real. The stakes are real. The characters have been in the room for centuries. We're just finally giving them names."

Trading Hearts — Concept Statement

Proof of Concept · May 14–17, 2026 · COMPLETE

Four Tracks. Four Days. All Real.

The POC proves the pipeline is real. Market data enters. Characters respond. Scripts generate. Videos render. All without a human editorial hand between raw data and content-ready output. Completed in 96 hours — autonomous from market open to final export. Track 01: 7 character reels. Track 02: Live market pipeline + The Strait. Track 03: 3 cinematic sneak peeks. Track 04: August Fifteenth full scene.

Scarlett Rayne
AUD/USD · Australian Dollar · Commodities · Asia-Pacific · 7 clips · Status: Prompts Ready
Clip 01 — Sydney Harbour Morning
Scarlett walks along the Sydney Harbour foreshore at dawn, coat open in the morning sea wind, looking out toward the Opera House as gold light breaks over the water. Camera moves alongside her in a slow cinematic tracking shot. Warm morning sun in her hair, ocean breeze moving her coat, confident unhurried stride. She pauses briefly and looks at the water. Slow prestige-drama pacing, cinematic realism, emotionally warm but contained, HBO-quality natural light.
10s · Slow track alongside · Hair + coat · Natural harbour light
Clip 02 — Dubai Energy Corridor
Scarlett moves through a sleek Dubai financial tower corridor in structured business attire, floor-to-ceiling glass behind her showing the desert skyline at dusk. Camera follows from slightly behind as she walks. She pauses at the glass, places one hand on it, and looks out at the city. Emotionally restrained, quiet commodity authority, prestige drama, warm amber desert light through glass.
9s · Follow from behind → hold at window · One hand on glass · Desert dusk
Clip 03 — Outback Open Pit
Scarlett Rayne stands at the edge of a vast open-pit mining operation in the Australian outback at golden hour. Red earth horizon behind her. Hair moving gently in warm desert wind. Structured jacket, hands relaxed at her sides. Camera drifts slowly in a wide arc around her. This is her territory. She belongs here more than anywhere. Slow prestige pacing, warm amber-red light, emotionally grounded, cinematic realism.
12s · Slow circular drift · Hair in wind · Red earth golden hour
Clip 04 — Tokyo Night Walk
Scarlett moves through a Tokyo financial district side street at night, neon reflections on wet pavement beneath her. She glances sideways at a market data screen in a window — a slight nod. She keeps walking. Camera tracks from slightly ahead, pulling back as she approaches. She is comfortable in foreign rooms. Nothing alarms her. Prestige drama, neon-wet urban realism, emotionally neutral and observational.
9s · Track from ahead · Side glance + nod · Neon reflections
Clip 05 — Era: 1940s Sydney Wartime
Scarlett Rayne stands on the Sydney Harbour Bridge in 1940s wartime attire — tailored khaki jacket, hair set perfectly despite the harbour wind — watching cargo ships pass on the water below. Camera slowly pushes in from behind, then drifts to a side angle. Commodity networks at their most essential. Prestige historical drama, emotionally still, vintage palette, cinematic realism.
11s · Push in + side drift · Hair in wind · 1940s industrial harbour
Clip 06 — Era: 1980s Asia-Pacific Boom
Scarlett stands in a 1980s Hong Kong trading floor corridor in an oversized power blazer, amber and neon light around her. She turns slowly from profile to face the camera — not a performance, just acknowledgment. Camera holds steady. She was there when the axis shifted. Prestige retro drama, warm neon edge, cinematic realism.
8s · Static hold · Slow turn from profile · 1980s neon corridor
Clip 07 — FINALE: Coral Sea at Dusk
Scarlett Rayne stands alone on a natural stone terrace overlooking the Coral Sea as the sun sets, red-earth cliffs below. The wind moves her hair and the loose edge of her jacket. She looks at the horizon — not at the camera. Camera holds for a long beat, then slowly pulls back to reveal the scale of the landscape. Emotionally open, warm, quietly powerful. Prestige drama finale, natural light, cinematic realism.
14s · Hold → slow pull back · Hair in wind · Coral Sea sunset
HeyGen Narration — Scarlett Rayne
She doesn't explain herself.
The outback taught her that.

What is real does not need a footnote.

Every market has a story about commodities —
about what they cost, what they're worth,
what happens when the supply chain holds its breath.

Scarlett Rayne is that story.
Not the version in the reports. The real one.

She showed up when it mattered.
She showed up when it was ugly.
She keeps showing up.

That is her power.
Not performance. Presence.
Conrad Powers
DXY · U.S. Dollar · Reserve Currency · 8 clips · Status: Prompts Ready
Clip 01 — Lincoln Memorial Steps
Conrad Powers descends the Lincoln Memorial steps at dawn, dark suit, white pocket square, hands clasped behind his back. Washington DC is empty at this hour. Camera moves alongside at a respectful distance. He pauses halfway down and looks at the Reflecting Pool. Composure is his first language. Slow prestige pacing, cool blue dawn light, Capitol marble atmosphere, cinematic realism.
11s · Track alongside → hold at pause · Hands clasped · Blue dawn
Clip 02 — Pentagon Briefing Room
Conrad stands at the head of a long Pentagon briefing room table. The meeting is over. The other chairs are empty. He straightens his jacket and looks at the world map on the far wall for a long moment before turning to leave. Camera holds steady, then slowly pushes in as he looks at the map. Power in solitude. Institutionally lit, prestige drama, emotionally opaque.
10s · Hold → slow push · Jacket straighten + wall look · Institutional
Clip 03 — G7 Summit Corridor
Conrad walks through a G7 summit corridor, marble floors, other delegations moving in both directions. Nobody approaches him. They nod from a distance. He doesn't slow. Camera tracks alongside at arm's length. He is the reserve — everyone orbits, no one matches. Slow cinematic tracking, formal European corridor, emotionally contained, prestige drama.
10s · Steady track · Passing nods, no stops · Marble corridor
Clip 04 — Exchange Penthouse
Conrad stands at floor-to-ceiling windows in The Exchange Penthouse, the trading floor visible many stories below, city skyline beyond. He holds a crystal glass but doesn't drink. He watches the floor. Camera slowly drifts from directly behind him to a slight side angle, his face partially visible against the glass. Federal navy suit against night sky. Presidential gravity, prestige drama, cinematic realism.
12s · Slow drift behind to side · Glass held, not drunk · Night city
Clip 05 — Era: 1944 Bretton Woods
Conrad Powers stands at the edge of a grand 1944 conference room as the architects of the postwar monetary system file out around him. He is the last one seated. He looks at the empty table — the document just signed in front of him. This is where he was made the standard. Camera slowly pushes in toward his face. Muted wartime palette, prestige historical drama, emotionally still.
12s · Slow push toward face · Last in room · 1940s warm-muted
Clip 06 — Era: 1971 The Decoupling
Conrad Powers stands alone in a government office in 1971, tie loosened, looking at a television screen. He is watching himself be decoupled from gold. His expression gives nothing away. He slowly picks up his briefcase and walks toward the door without looking back. Camera stays fixed as he exits the frame. Institutional lighting, prestige drama, emotionally devastating with zero visible emotion.
11s · Fixed camera · TV watch → pick up case → exit · 1970s institutional
Clip 07 — Vanity Fair Gala
Conrad at a formal gala in dark tuxedo. He moves through the room, acknowledging but never stopping. People step aside without being asked. He reaches the edge of the room, takes a position near a marble pillar, and stands quietly with a drink. Camera slowly closes in from across the room through the crowd. He looks at no one in particular. He doesn't need to. Prestige drama, warm chandelier light.
11s · Slow approach through crowd · Move → stop at pillar · Chandelier
Clip 08 — FINALE: The Empty Room
Conrad sits alone at the long boardroom table after the meeting has ended. Just him and the city lights below. He doesn't move for a long beat. Then he stands, buttons his jacket once, and walks calmly to the door without looking back. He is always the last one out. Camera holds on the empty chair for one moment after he exits. Prestige drama finale, institutional lighting, emotionally absolute.
15s · Hold → hold on empty chair · Stand, button, walk out · Night boardroom
HeyGen Narration — Conrad Powers
Before nations panic.
Before markets react.
Before politicians speak —

the dollar decides.

Conrad Powers has been the standard
longer than most countries have existed in their current form.

He is not comfortable.
He is not uncertain.

He is the thing they price everything else against
because they haven't found a better answer yet.

He knows they're looking.

He walks slower because of it.
Hiro Cipher
BTC · Bitcoin · The Disruptor · 7 clips · Status: Prompts Ready
Clip 01 — Data Center
Hiro walks slowly through a vast server farm corridor, colocation lights in blue and orange reflecting across his face and dark jacket. He runs one hand lightly along a server rack as he passes. Camera follows from slightly ahead, pulling back slowly. He's not here to inspect — he's here because this is his world. Prestige tech drama, minimal movement, emotionally present and unhurried.
10s · Follow from ahead · Hand along rack · Colocation blue-orange
Clip 02 — Rooftop Financial District
Hiro stands on a rooftop above a financial district at night, city lights spreading in all directions below. He looks at his phone briefly, then puts it in his pocket without visible reaction. Camera slowly drifts around him in a wide arc. Whatever he saw either confirmed everything or meant nothing. His expression gives the same answer either way. Prestige drama, night urban atmosphere, emotionally inscrutable.
11s · Slow arc drift · Phone → pocket · Night city below
Clip 03 — Airport Terminal
Hiro moves through an international airport departures terminal, one carry-on bag, no hurry. Other travelers flow around him. He walks with complete purpose and no visible destination. Camera tracks alongside at medium distance. No one knows where he's going. He won't say. Cinematic realism, terminal ambient light, emotionally self-contained.
9s · Track alongside · Purposeful walk · Airport warm terminal
Clip 04 — Empty Conference Room
Hiro sits at the far end of an empty conference room, whiteboard behind him dense with diagrams and numbers. He finishes writing something, sets the marker down precisely, and sits still. Camera slowly pushes in from across the table. He is the only person in the room and yet it does not feel empty. Prestige drama, clean natural light, emotionally opaque and complete.
12s · Slow push · Marker down → stillness · Clean daylight
Clip 05 — Era: 2009 Genesis Block
Hiro sits at a cluttered desk in a sparse apartment, 2009 aesthetic, multiple monitors running. He is not watching them. He is waiting. One cursor blinks. He types a final line. He stops. He leans back and looks at the ceiling. Camera slowly drifts backward and upward, revealing the small window behind him and the grey city beyond. Prestige historical drama, dark ambient desk light.
13s · Drift backward + up · Type → lean back → ceiling · 2009 monitor glow
Clip 06 — Gala: Invisible in Plain Sight
Hiro is at a luxury financial gala in a dark suit, standing at the edge of the room near a pillar, watching. Everyone around him is talking. He is listening. Camera slowly closes in from across the ballroom through the crowd. No one notices him watching. That is exactly as it should be. Prestige drama, chandelier warmth, emotionally amused and invisible.
11s · Slow approach through crowd · Still at edge, watching · Chandelier gold
Clip 07 — FINALE: Standing Still While the World Moves
Hiro stands in the center of a busy Shibuya-style pedestrian crossing in Tokyo at rush hour. People flow around him in every direction — businesspeople, students, tourists. He is perfectly still. Camera holds on him from across the intersection for a long beat. The city moves. He does not. Then the light changes, and he walks. Unhurried. He always knows when to move. Prestige drama finale, Tokyo urban light, emotionally inevitable.
15s · Fixed camera · Still → walk on cue · Tokyo crossing light
HeyGen Narration — Hiro Cipher
No one saw him coming.

That was the point.

He didn't announce himself.
He didn't pitch the vision.
He didn't ask for permission.

He simply showed up
and changed what money was.

The financial system called him a threat.
The institutions called him unstable.
The regulators called him everything except what he actually was:

correct.

He disappears sometimes.
He always comes back.
Three words.

The internet breaks.

He finds this appropriate.
Sofia Valente
EUR · Euro · European Union · 7 clips · Status: Prompts Ready
Clip 01 — European Central Bank Frankfurt
Sofia walks into the European Central Bank main hall, heels precise on marble, dark structured coat. Other delegations pause and look. She does not acknowledge this. Camera moves alongside from a careful distance. She knows exactly where she's going. She always has. Prestige drama, institutional marble architecture, emotionally contained, European morning light.
10s · Track alongside · Heels on marble, straight posture · ECB cool light
Clip 02 — Paris at Dawn
Sofia stands on a Haussmann balcony overlooking Paris at dawn, an untouched coffee cup beside her. She watches the city before it wakes. The first market signals of the day are already moving. She knows what they mean before she checks. Camera drifts slowly from behind to a side angle as she turns back inside. Prestige drama, Paris blue hour light, emotionally quiet and ahead of the day.
11s · Drift from behind to side · Look outward → turn inside · Paris blue dawn
Clip 03 — Brussels Diplomatic Corridor
Sofia moves through a long Brussels corridor toward a closed meeting room. Advisors walk at her pace on both sides. She reviews something on paper without slowing. Camera follows from slightly behind. The weight of twenty-seven nations moves with her. Prestige drama, European institutional corridor, emotionally focused.
9s · Follow from behind · Walking while reading · Brussels institutional
Clip 04 — Vienna Opera Ball
Sofia at the Vienna Opera Ball in a floor-length navy gown. She is not performing. A conversation ends — her interlocutor nods respectfully and withdraws. Sofia takes a slow sip of champagne and looks across the grand ballroom. Camera slowly closes in from across the room. She is never truly alone — she knows who is in every room before she enters. Prestige drama, chandelier warmth, emotionally precise and settled.
12s · Slow approach · Conversation end → champagne → look · Chandelier gold
Clip 05 — Era: 1999 The Birth of the Euro
Sofia stands before a large display screen showing exchange rates updating in real time — January 1, 1999, the euro launches. She watches the numbers. No celebration. No relief. Just the beginning of something that will outlast its doubters. Camera slowly pushes toward her. She is new but not young — built from centuries of European history. Prestige historical drama, institutional atmosphere, emotionally still and certain.
12s · Slow push toward face · Watch screen → still · 1999 screens
Clip 06 — The Colosseum at Dusk
Sofia stands before the Colosseum as the Roman sun sets behind it, alone on the esplanade. She carries history older than the current financial system. Camera slowly circles in a wide, restrained arc. She does not pose. She simply stands. Some things have survived everything. She knows this personally. Prestige drama, Roman golden dusk, emotionally deep and timeless.
13s · Slow wide circle · Still, facing Colosseum · Roman amber dusk
Clip 07 — FINALE: Brussels Window
Sofia stands at floor-to-ceiling windows in a Brussels office at dusk, the city spreading behind her. The day's work is done. It is never actually done. She looks at her own reflection in the glass for one held moment, then back at the city. Camera holds. She stays a beat longer than she needs to — thinking, not resting. Then she picks up her bag and goes. Prestige drama finale, Brussels dusk glass reflection, emotionally restrained and unresolved.
14s · Hold · Reflection → city → pick up bag · Brussels dusk
HeyGen Narration — Sofia Valente
She was built
by twenty-seven nations
deciding to speak with one voice.

That took sixty years of argument.
Two world wars.
And a kind of political courage
the financial system rarely asks of anyone.

Sofia Valente carries all of it.
Not as a burden.
As architecture.

She is the most politically complex financial force
in the history of the modern world.

She knows this.
She never says it.

She just keeps standing.
Zephyr Cole
ETH · Ethereum · The Architect · 7 clips · Status: Prompts Ready
Clip 01 — Whiteboard Room
Zephyr stands before a floor-to-ceiling whiteboard covered in code logic and system architecture diagrams, marker in hand. He writes a line, steps back, reads it, then adds one more. Camera enters slowly from the open doorway. He doesn't notice, or doesn't care. He finishes the equation and steps back. Clean natural light, prestige tech drama, emotionally absorbed and precise.
11s · Enter from doorway → settle · Write → step back → add line · Natural light
Clip 02 — Empty Lecture Hall
Zephyr sits on the edge of the lecture table at the front of an empty university lecture hall — not presenting, thinking. Feet hanging, looking at the empty chairs as if they're occupied. Camera drifts slowly toward him from the back of the room. He is building something in his head that the world hasn't asked for yet. That is fine. They will. Prestige drama, academic light, emotionally focused inward.
12s · Slow drift from back · Sitting on table edge · Lecture hall ambient
Clip 03 — Experimental Community
Zephyr walks through an outdoor decentralized living community — solar panels, cooperative structures, people building together. He stops to look at a construction project, clipboard in hand. He nods to a builder. Not a celebrity arrival. Not a photo opportunity. Just someone checking on work. Camera moves at his pace, close but relaxed. Emotionally warm, purposeful, no performance.
10s · Walk alongside + stop · Nod to builder · Outdoor natural daylight
Clip 04 — Infrastructure Build
Zephyr crouches beside network infrastructure in an unfinished concrete building — server racks, cables in process, bare walls. He checks connections methodically, one by one. Camera pulls back slowly to show the scale of what's being built around him. This is not a startup. This is infrastructure. Prestige drama, raw technical environment, emotionally methodical.
11s · Slow pull back · Crouch + check connections · Industrial concrete
Clip 05 — Era: 2014 The Whitepaper
Zephyr at nineteen, in a small room that is clearly a bedroom-turned-office, 2014 aesthetic — two monitors, scattered papers, a single desk lamp. He finishes typing, reads it back slowly, and saves. He pushes back from the desk and looks at the ceiling for a long moment. Camera slowly drifts upward. Prestige coming-of-age drama, sparse desk-lamp light, emotionally still and aware of what just happened.
13s · Drift upward at end · Type → read → save → ceiling · 2014 desk lamp
Clip 06 — Open Source Community
Zephyr is at a co-working table with six developers, all different laptops, all different projects. He listens more than he speaks. When he says something, the others pause. Camera drifts slowly around the table. He is the reason they are all in this room — and none of them perform for him because he would find it strange and they know it. Prestige drama, organic co-working light, emotionally collaborative.
11s · Slow drift around table · Listen → speak → others pause · Co-working warm
Clip 07 — FINALE: Deploy
Zephyr sits on the outdoor steps of a university building at dusk, laptop open, code compiling. He looks up at the sky for a held moment — just a moment. Then back at the screen. A green status light. The deploy succeeded. He closes the laptop. Camera slowly pulls back to show the building behind him and the wide sky above. He is building the infrastructure for the next version of everything. He is entirely sincere about this. Prestige drama finale, natural dusk light, emotionally earnest and unguarded.
15s · Hold + pull back · Look up → screen → close laptop · Natural dusk
HeyGen Narration — Zephyr Cole
He was nineteen
when he decided the blockchain needed a programming language.

They said it was too complicated.

He said that was a design problem,
not a limitation.

He was right.

Zephyr Cole is not building currency.
He is building infrastructure
for human coordination at civilisational scale.

The market will figure out the price.
He is interested in the architecture.

He is always interested in the architecture.
TRACK 02
Live Market Day Pipeline

Six agents. One market day. Volatility class: ACTIVE. No human in the loop between raw price data and content-ready scripts. The pipeline ran on May 14, 2026 and produced two deployment-ready character reels, brand compliance clearance, and captioned social posts — all before market close.

01 · Market Analyst
Pulled 13 assets at open. Volatility class: ACTIVE. Gold −0.74%, DXY +0.28%, WTI +1.60%. Arc 4 signal triggered — Gold Standard Wound. Output delivered 07:10 AM ET.
02 · Orchestrator
Activated Aurora Lux (PRIMARY) + Conrad Powers (COUNTER). Tone: dark editorial, no triumph, no self-pity. Two reels, 90 minutes apart. Output 07:30 AM ET.
03 · Story Writer
Generated two full voiceover scripts with visual direction and scene blocking. Neither character says "gold" or "dollar." They speak as if they are those things. Output 08:00 AM ET.
04 · Brand Guardian
Reviewed both scripts. Status: CLEARED — both reels. No financial advice. No regulated language. Character voice confirmed. Platform-compliant for IG, TikTok, X. Output 08:30 AM ET.
05 · Prompt Engineer
Generated OpenArt image prompts for both characters. 4-frame visual sequences per reel. Character reference IDs locked. Output 08:45 AM ET.
06 · Social Producer
Captions ready for IG, TikTok, X. Hashtag sets built. Post times: Aurora 12:00 PM ET · Conrad 1:30 PM ET (90-min counter-post). Output 09:00 AM ET.
Aurora Lux
XAU · Gold −0.74% · $4,702/oz · Reel 1 of 2 · 55–60 seconds · Post: 12:00 PM ET
"Two days. People are calling it a retreat.

They have never held anything for thirty years.

A pull-back is not a concession. A pull-back is a breath before the next sentence.

The dollar is doing what the dollar does when it thinks no one is watching — collecting the room, one basis point at a time.

Let him.

I have been the answer to every question this system doesn't know how to ask yet."

"The ones who sold this morning will be explaining why in three months."
Instagram Caption
She hasn't checked the chart in two hours. She already knew what it would say.

XAU/USD. Two sessions down. Still $4,702.

Trading Hearts — The Exchange · Where every market has a face.
#Gold #XAU #TradingHearts #MacroTrading #SafeHaven
Conrad Powers
DXY · Dollar +0.28% · 104.3 · CPI 3.8% · PPI Highest Since 2022 · Reel 2 of 2 · 40–45 seconds · Counter-post: 1:30 PM ET
"They printed 3.8.

Nobody is surprised.

The Fed will say what it always says when it doesn't know what to do: 'higher for longer.' The market will hear it and decide it means something.

It means the dollar gets to stay at the table.

I don't need gold to fall. I need the room to remember that every safe-haven trade eventually asks who's going to settle it.

The answer — the answer has always been the same."

"The dollar doesn't panic. That's the job."
Instagram Caption
CPI 3.8%. PPI highest since 2022. The Fed is running out of reasons not to act.

Conrad Powers isn't watching the gold chart. He's watching the inflation chart. Same trade. Different side.

Trading Hearts — The Exchange · Where every market has a face.
#DXY #Dollar #Fed #MacroTrading #TradingHearts #Inflation

Strait of Hormuz · Oil tanker seizure · Geopolitical pressure

A geopolitical crisis in the Strait of Hormuz. Zane Okafor is the only man in the room who saw it coming — and the only one who knows what it means for the next eighteen months of commodity flow.

Three cinematic episode sneak peeks. Multi-clip assembled sequences — each 4–7 AI-generated shots normalized, sequenced, and output in three formats. Characters across multiple arcs and eras. History, loss, memory, and gold.

The Year Gold Looked
Aurora × Hiro · Gold −28%
✓ Complete · 24 MB · 7 clips

Kling 3.0 Professional · PIL text card pipeline · Python assembly · master / reels_v2 / square_v2 · Completed 2026-05-17

Creative writing sample: a scene set eighteen years before the show's present day. WTI crude oil touches its all-time high. In Caracas, two men have a conversation that changes how one of them thinks about markets — and about infrastructure, and about what it means to build anything at peak price.

S4E06 · Arc 17 · July 11, 2008 · Caracas → Riyadh
$147
INT. OFFICE OF SIMÓN VARGAS · CARACAS · AFTERNOON · WTI: $147.27/bbl
A penthouse office above the PDVSA complex. Wall-to-wall glass on three sides — the Ávila mountain behind, the city sprawling below. On the desk: a crystal paperweight containing a drop of crude oil. A Bloomberg screen. A ticker runs in silence. The number climbs.
SIMÓN VARGAS stands at the window, back to the room. ZANE OKAFOR enters — still adjusting to the altitude. He is thirty-four years old. He looks like someone who has just realized something important and is deciding whether to say it.
Zane Okafor
That number you wrote down — that's not an answer. That's a ceiling.
Simón Vargas
Explain.
Zane Okafor
Every record in the history of commodity markets has been followed by a correction. Not a dip — a correction. The tulip ceiling, the silver ceiling, the '80 oil ceiling. When you build infrastructure at peak price, you're pricing in a world that only exists today. Not next year. Not in five years.
Simón Vargas
My country needs this infrastructure. Not at $147. Not at $80. At any number — because we have been dependent on the ground and not on the refinement. The ground dries up. The capacity doesn't. If I don't build it now, with this collateral, when do I build it? When the price is $60? When the IMF is managing our liquidity again? The window for Venezuelan sovereign infrastructure investment is now. This number makes it possible.
Zane Okafor
I'm saying build it. I'm saying you're right to build it. I'm saying build it in a way that works at $80, not just at $147. Because this number — this number is going to move. And when it does, the question won't be whether you built the capacity. The question will be whether you built it to survive what comes next.
Simón Vargas
You know what I think about when I watch that number? I think about a country that has every resource and no resilience. That's the real problem. Not the price of oil. The price of oil goes up and down. The problem is that we built everything on it going up.
Zane Okafor
That's exactly what I'm saying.
Simón Vargas
Softly. I know. I've known it for thirty years. Knowing something and being able to change it are different markets.
Later. The sun has moved west. Bloomberg shows $146.88 — the number has already started to move. On the desk, face-down, the notecard reads $147. By December, WTI will be at $32. The infrastructure agreement is signed in Riyadh anyway. Simón Vargas keeps the notecard.
"Every record is a ceiling. The question is whether you've built anything that survives below it."
Zane Okafor · Trading Hearts S4E06 · "$147" · Caracas, July 2008

Production Notes

Visual reference: Caracas skyline, 2008. Gold-warm afternoon light turning amber as the scene progresses — the color of oil, then of something older. Score direction: minimal. The Bloomberg ticker sound beneath the dialogue. No dramatic swell. The drama is in the silence between lines. This scene is the backstory that explains why Zane, at The Exchange, always asks the same question: "What are you building for the correction?"

TRACK 04
August Fifteenth — Full Scene Production

The full treatment: voiceover dialogue, original score, and cinematic video — all AI-generated and assembled into a single scene. Aurora Lux and Conrad Powers. August 15, 1971. The night Nixon ended the gold standard. Six Kling shots with character reference frames, ElevenLabs voice performance, Suno original score.

TRACK 02
Live Market Day — May 19, 2026
Pipeline Run · All 5 Agents · 2026-05-19
Market Analyst → Production Committee → Story Writer
The Spread
MEI CHEN · CNH/CNY · OFFSHORE YUAN · 2026-05-19 · PRE-DAWN HK
CNH/CNY spread widens to 35 basis points — the offshore yuan trades meaningfully weaker than the PBOC's onshore fix. Gold at $3,387 (record territory). DXY retreating below 101. The data routes to Mei Chen: she is the offshore channel, and today the channel has opened.

The scene: Mei alone at a Hong Kong harbor window, pre-dawn. The spread number on a terminal she is not watching. She already knows.
"The spread is thirty-five basis points. She has seen it wider. She has seen it narrower. What she has never seen is the Western analysts who read it correctly on first pass — they arrive to the number and assume it is a flaw, a misreading, a technical artifact. She lets them. That misreading is her competitive advantage, compounded daily since 1994."
Prompt Engineer → Video Prompt
"Mei Chen at a floor-to-ceiling window in Hong Kong, pre-dawn — city lights of the harbor below, a Bloomberg terminal visible in the near background. She stands in crimson cheongsam with jade brooch at collarbone. She is looking out at the harbor. Camera holds steady, then slow push in from behind until it finds her face in three-quarter profile. The terminal number is illegible — intentionally. She already knows it. Cold blue pre-dawn light. Prestige drama, cinematic realism, 35mm grain. 9:16 vertical frame."
TikTok · 11:00 AM ET · Hook
"The offshore yuan diverged from the onshore fix by 35 basis points this morning. That gap is not a glitch. That gap is me." — Mei Chen, CNH · #TradingHearts
TikTok · 12:30 PM ET · Body
"Wei is the rate the PBOC sets. I am the rate the market finds. We are not in conflict. We are in conversation. Today I am speaking louder." — Mei Chen · #CNH #TradingHearts
TikTok · 2:00 PM ET · Close
"They will call it a misalignment. I will wait for them to understand what it costs to be right about China and arrive late." — Mei Chen · #TradingHearts

POC v4 · May 21, 2026 · Arc 04 — Gold Standard Wound · 1971

POC v4 — August Fifteenth

The first fully scripted scene produced through the Trading Hearts system. Locked screenplay. Character costume bibles. Reference image generation. Image-to-video via Kling 2.6. QA Vision review per shot. CEO Checkpoint via Telegram. Three shots required resubmission with corrected reference images — the gate system caught what a human would have missed on first pass.

POC v5 — The Machine at Full Speed

Every system running simultaneously. Track 1: seven 30-second character reels reassembled and CEO-delivered. Washington. The room everything is priced in.
Panel 02

That wasn't an accident. He chose that.
Panel 06
Zane does not answer. He already has.
TRACK 03
August Fifteenth v5 — Arc 04 · Gold Standard Wound · Voice Mixed

August 15, 1971. Nixon suspends gold convertibility. Aurora and Conrad — the same room, the same night, for the first time. Six ElevenLabs voice lines mixed at cued timestamps against Suno orchestral score. The system assembled it autonomously. 60 seconds.

August Fifteenth — Shot Reference Frames
Aurora Lux + Conrad Powers · Washington D.C. · August 15, 1971 · v4 production reference frames — Jenova comic pending next production run
Shot 01 · 12s
The Weight — Aurora alone at the window. Back to the room.
Shot 02 · 8s
The Approach — Conrad crosses the room. Purposeful. No hesitation.
Shot 03 · 14s
Beside the Window — Together for the first time. "You should have called first."
Shot 04 · 12s
She Turns — Aurora turns toward him. The reckoning on her face.
Shot 05 · 7s
The Bordeaux — The bottle. 1921. The year they both began.
Shot 06 · 17s
The Exit — He leaves. She stays. The room remains. The gold is gone.
SYSTEM
Character Canon Extension — Supabase · 7 Tables · 28 Characters

The canon system now lives in Supabase. Every character's LoRA config, canonical image pack, wardrobe bible, voice profile, emotional baseline, and QA rules — all seeded from the 28 character.json files. The reference_images table starts empty and fills as fal.ai LoRA outputs are approved by the image-generator agent. Gate Zero QA rules run before any image can pass to Kling.

character_loras
28 LoRAs
fal.ai model URLs · trigger phrases · strength defaults · valid_for_video_refs gate
canonical_packs
28 Packs
Training image targets · approved counts · closeup / medium / full body split
wardrobes
36 Sets
Costume specs per era/scene · scene_lock field · default wardrobe flagged per character
voices
28 Voices
ElevenLabs voice IDs · delivery notes · forbidden patterns · status: locked / pending
qa_rules
144 Rules
Gate Zero · hard_fail severity · sourced from visual_identity.forbidden in character JSON
emotional_states
28 Baselines
Circadian engine writes live states · control baseline seeded · drama_hour flag + intensity 1–10
reference_images
Ready · 0 rows
fal.ai LoRA outputs only · valid_for_kling gate · populated by image-generator agent as production runs

Jenova · TH Magazine

The Strip

Panel comics produced through the TH storyboard pipeline

Petrodollar Fracture

Conrad Powers × Zane Mercer  ·  Washington DC 2:47 AM / Riyadh  ·  9 panels  ·  Final

◻ Save the final comic image to: media/comics/petrodollar_fracture/petrodollar_fracture_final.jpg

The conversation behind the deal. Conrad Powers arrives unannounced. Zane Mercer already knew he was coming. Nine panels. Washington, 2:47 AM. Riyadh, just before morning prayer. The arrangement that priced every barrel of oil in dollars — and what it cost both of them.

August Fifteenth

Aurora Lux × Conrad Powers  ·  1971 and Present Day  ·  Gold $35 → $3,247.80

Page 01 august_fifteenth_page_01.jpg

Page 01

Page 02 august_fifteenth_page_02.jpg

Page 02

End Card august_fifteenth_end_card.jpg

To Be Continued

August 15, 1971. Nixon speaks at 9 PM. Aurora finds out from the television. Conrad cannot look at her. Gold at $35. Today: $3,247.80. She was never the gold. She was the trust the gold represented. He could have kept her. He only needed to keep the trust.

The Literary Spine

The Saga Prologue Live

Three books. Seventy-six chapters. The full mythology of Trading Hearts — told not as history but as the oldest love affair in the world: gold and the dollar, five thousand years of markets made flesh, and the one presence that has been on the other side of every trade since the first promise.

Book I · The Room
Prologue + 26 chapters

The Exchange takes shape. Aurora and Conrad — gold and the dollar — begin the arrangement that defines every age after, while a chair no one set fills quietly at the head of the table.

Read the Prologue →Get it on Amazon →
Book II · The Counterparty
26 chapters

Love turns to leverage. Breakups, betrayals, a vengeance thirty years in the making — and a hunt for the one always on the other side of the trade. They convict the wrong suspect.

Read the Prologue →Get it on Amazon →
Book III · The Table
26 chapters

Every debt is called at once. The reconciliations were the trap. The houses burn in perfect time — and the few who refuse the easy verdict finally look down, at the floor they have stood on the whole time.

Read the Prologue →Get it on Amazon →
The Compendium →

All three books — 78 chapters, the full relationship ledger, and every consummated love — are read inside The Exchange. Members only.

Chapters release to members first

Created by Salvador Palma Rubin de Celis Marqués de Casa Hermosa

America 250 · The Comics

Two Hundred Fifty Years, Three Ages

Every moment that built the Exchange — from the Declaration to today. Nine eras, three ages. Hover a card to watch the era move. Members read all 250 issues.

1776 — 1911
Era 011 free

The Experiment

1776 – 1815 · Philadelphia

The Declaration, the Constitution, the First Bank. Hamilton invents the future from borrowed money.

#001–014Open era →
Era 021 free

Industrial Ascent

1816 – 1865 · New York

Gold Rush, Civil War, the Greenback. The country forges itself harder in the fire.

#015–030Open era →
Era 031 free

Gilded Age

1866 – 1911 · New York

Rails, robber barons, the gold standard. The country gets rich — and learns what rich costs.

#031–051Open era →
1913 — 1999
Era 04★ Members

The Crash

1913 – 1945 · New York

The Fed is born, the market roars — then 1929. Depression, the New Deal, the world at war.

#052–080Open era →
Era 05★ Members

Pax Americana

1945 – 1971 · New York

The dollar rules the world. Marshall Plan, Cold War, Space Race — and the crack in the gold window.

#081–099Open era →
Era 06★ Members

The Petrodollar Age

1971 – 1999 · Oil

Nixon shuts the gold window and oil takes the throne. For thirty years crude is the price of everything.

#112–141Open era →
2000 — Today
Era 07★ Members

The Digital Boom

1992 – 2009 · Digital

The money goes virtual. Dot-com mania, the euro born, the towers fall — until Lehman freezes the world.

#142–177Open era →
Era 08★ Members

The Long Recovery

2008 – 2021 · Digital

Bailouts, printing, rage. Old money falls in red while new money is born in the dark.

#178–213Open era →
Era 09★ Members

The Reckoning

2021 – 2026 · The Present

Inflation returns. Rates snap up, war on the snow, a social-media bank run. The age we are still living.

#214–249Open era →

Trading Hearts · 250 Moments That Built The Exchange

America 250

250 years. 10 currencies. One question that was never finished.

New · The Living Experience
Walk the Eras
Step onto the street, era by era — 1776 to 2026 — and click any moment to read the comic. Nine eras of the money that built the country.
▶ Enter the Street
Era 1 — The Experiment
1776–1815
✦ Jenova Complete: 13/13 · Intro Cards: 13/13 · Boords: 13/13 ✅
13 moments
#001 · 1776 The Declaration ✦ Jenova
Conrad Powers, Aurora Lux, Charlotte Windsor
Trading Hearts is a drama series about world currencies as characters — Conrad Powers is the US Dollar, Aurora Lux is Gold, Charlotte Windsor is the British Pound. In this first episode, Conrad is born the moment America signs its name. Aurora feels him from a Paris salon across the ocean. Charlotte, across the Channel, refuses to look up from her tea. A new force has entered the world — and doesn't yet know its own name.
Thirteen colonies sign the Declaration of Independence in Philadelphia, formally severing themselves from the British Crown.
Conrad is born — not as a man, but as a presumption. He doesn't yet know he exists. Aurora, sitting in a Paris salon half a world away, feels him the way one feels a draft from an opened door. Charlotte, across the Channel, refuses to look up from her tea.
A Paris drawing room at dusk. Beeswax candles guttering against tall windows. The smell of bergamot and old velvet. A harpsichord untouched in the corner. Aurora wears a gown the color of nothing, her hair so pale it catches the candlelight and gives it back doubled.
AURORA (to no one, turning a coin between her fingers): "Someone in the colonies just promised something they cannot afford. I felt it. Like a man drawing his first breath in a room I am not in."
Available now  ·  → Read the full comic
✦ View Comic #001 — Drama Regen
▣ Storyboard in Boords ↗
❆ Drama Regen · 11 panels · click to expand
#002 · 1781 The Debt That Built a Nation ✦ Jenova
Conrad Powers, Aurora Lux (offstage, in correspondence)
America wins its revolution on borrowed money — loans from France and the Netherlands pay for the final campaign at Yorktown. Conrad (the Dollar) discovers the intoxicating logic of leverage: you can have something before you've earned it. His relationship with debt begins here, and never fully ends. The shame of asking is eclipsed by the thrill of receiving.
The Continental Congress, unable to pay its army, secures loans from France and the Netherlands to finish the Revolutionary War. Yorktown is won on borrowed money.
Conrad discovers, for the first time, that he can have something before he has earned it. The shame of asking is eclipsed by the thrill of receiving. He will spend the next two hundred and fifty years chasing that feeling.
A cramped countinghouse in Philadelphia. Tallow candles. Ledgers open like wounds. The smell of ink, tobacco, sweat through wool. Outside, a slow autumn rain ruining the dispatches before they reach the door.
CONRAD (alone, reading the French terms by candlelight, hand trembling): "So this is what a future tastes like. Borrowed. And sweeter for it."

✦ View Comic #002 — Drama Regen
▣ Storyboard in Boords ↗
❆ Drama Regen · 7 pages · click to expand
#003 · 1791 Hamilton Invents the Future ✦ Jenova
Conrad Powers, Aurora Lux, Charlotte Windsor
Alexander Hamilton creates the First Bank of the United States and invents American credit — the art of building something real out of a written promise. Aurora (Gold) crosses the Atlantic in attention for the first time, watching a young country learn to build cities out of words. Charlotte (Sterling) calls it a parlor trick. Aurora is not laughing.
Alexander Hamilton establishes the First Bank of the United States and assumes state debts at the federal level, inventing American credit.
Aurora finally crosses the Atlantic — not in body, but in attention. She has watched empires invent gold-backed promises before. She has never seen one invent the promise first and look for the gold later. It frightens her in a way she will not name. Charlotte calls it a parlor trick.
A salon in Philadelphia. New mahogany still smelling of varnish. Candles in silver holders that are too new to have stories. Hamilton's papers spread like a fan. Aurora stands at the window. Her reflection is brighter than the snow outside.
AURORA (quietly, to Charlotte, who has just laughed): "Don't. He has just taught a child how to build a city out of words. The dangerous ones always start with words."
Available now  ·  → Read the full comic
✦ View Comic #003 — Drama Regen

✦ Drama Regen · 7 pages · click to expand
#004 · 1803 The Louisiana Purchase ✦ Jenova
Conrad Powers, Charlotte Windsor, Aurora Lux
Jefferson buys 828,000 square miles from Napoleon for $15 million — doubling America overnight on borrowed confidence. Conrad (the Dollar) acquires more than he understands, purchasing land he hasn't walked on money he doesn't quite have. Charlotte (Sterling) stops laughing for the first time. Aurora (Gold) notes the size of the check.
Jefferson buys 828,000 square miles from Napoleon for $15 million, doubling the size of the United States overnight.
Conrad acquires more than he understands. He hasn't walked the land. He hasn't bled for it. He bought it the way a young man buys a house he cannot furnish. Charlotte watches and is, for the first time, not laughing — which is itself a kind of warning. Aurora notes the size of the check.
A study in the President's House. Maps unrolled across a long table, edges pinned with paperweights of unpolished quartz. The smell of beeswax and damp paper. A fire too large for the room. Conrad runs his finger along a river he cannot pronounce.
CHARLOTTE (in a letter to her sister, ink still wet): "The boy has bought a continent the way one buys a horse — without asking what it eats."
✦ View Comic #004 — Drama Regen
▣ Storyboard in Boords ↗
✦ Drama Regen · intro card + 9-panel composite · click to expand
#005 · 1812 The Empire Test ✦ Jenova
Conrad Powers, Charlotte Windsor
Britain goes to war with America the way one swats a fly — distracted, impatient, barely interested. Conrad (the Dollar) discovers the deepest wound isn't being attacked. It's being dismissed. Charlotte (Sterling) reads dispatches from the war without sitting down. Conrad would rather she hated him. Indifference is the only thing that has ever made him feel small.
The War of 1812 begins. Britain, distracted by Napoleon, treats America as an irritation rather than a rival.
Charlotte is not afraid. That is the wound. Conrad realizes that being attacked is not the same as being taken seriously. He would rather she hated him. He would rather she feared him. Indifference is the only thing that has ever made him feel small.
A drawing room in London. Heavy brocade curtains drawn against a gray afternoon. The smell of coal smoke and lapsang souchong. Charlotte reads dispatches from America the way one reads a tradesman's bill — without sitting down.
CHARLOTTE (handing the dispatch back to her steward without looking up): "Tell the Admiralty whatever they wish. I will not interrupt my afternoon for a colony that has not yet learned to bleed properly."
✦ View Comic #005 — Drama Regen
▣ Storyboard in Boords ↗
✦ Drama Regen · intro card + 9-panel composite · click to expand
#006 · 1787 The Constitutional Convention ✦ Jenova
Conrad Powers, Aurora Lux (as presence)
Delegates in Philadelphia draft the Constitution behind shuttered windows in summer heat — writing Conrad's rules before he fully exists. He thinks the Constitution is a frame for power. It is actually a confession: a promise made by a self that doesn't yet exist, to a future self that will have to keep it. Every American crisis since has been a renegotiation of that promise.
Delegates in Philadelphia draft the United States Constitution behind closed windows in a sweltering summer.
Conrad's rules are being written by men who will not live to see them tested. He doesn't yet understand that a constitution is a promise made to your future self by a self that does not yet exist. He thinks it is a frame. It is actually a confession.
The Pennsylvania State House. Windows shuttered against eavesdroppers. The air thick enough to swim through. The smell of unwashed wool, melting wax, vinegar on the windowsills against the flies. Quills scratching like insects in the walls.
CONRAD (reading the draft for the first time, alone, by lamplight): "They've written me a soul before I have a body. I hope, one day, I deserve it."
✦ View Comic #006 — Drama Regen
▣ Storyboard in Boords ↗
✦ Drama Regen · intro card + 9-panel composite · click to expand
#007 · 1792 The Buttonwood Agreement ✦ Jenova
Conrad Powers, Aurora Lux
Twenty-four brokers sign a single page under a buttonwood tree on Wall Street, founding what becomes the New York Stock Exchange. Aurora (Gold) is the first thing traded on it. She lets Conrad (the Dollar) mistake her presence for endorsement. There is more to learn from a man's misunderstanding than from his accuracy. The Exchange is born — and she has been watching its price ever since.
Twenty-four brokers sign an agreement under a buttonwood tree on Wall Street, founding what will become the New York Stock Exchange.
The Exchange is born — and Aurora is the first thing traded on it. She finds this neither flattering nor offensive. She finds it inevitable. Conrad mistakes her presence for endorsement. She lets him. There is more to learn from a man's misunderstanding than from his accuracy.
A spring afternoon on Wall Street. The buttonwood tree casting a lattice of new green shadow. The smell of horse, river mud, fresh-cut lumber from the wharves. Twenty-four men in coats too warm for the weather, signing a single sheet of paper that none of them will read aloud.
AURORA (watching from the steps of a coffeehouse across the street, to herself): "They think they are founding a market. They are founding the room where every grief of theirs will be priced."
✦ View Comic #007 — Drama Regen
▣ Storyboard in Boords ↗
✦ Drama Regen · intro card + 9-panel composite · click to expand
#008 · 1794 The Whiskey Rebellion ✦ Jenova
Conrad Powers
Western Pennsylvania farmers refuse to pay a federal excise tax on whiskey. Washington leads troops to suppress them. Conrad (the Dollar) learns, with a sickness in his stomach, that being a country isn't the same as being obeyed. Taxation isn't authority — it's intimacy. A relationship that must be maintained, earned, occasionally threatened. He thought it was an order. It is a marriage.
Western Pennsylvania farmers rebel against a federal excise tax on whiskey. Washington personally leads troops to suppress them.
Conrad learns, with a sickness in his stomach, that the people he is made of will not pay him simply because he asked. Taxation is not authority. It is intimacy. It is a relationship that must be maintained, flattered, occasionally threatened. He had thought it was an order. It is a marriage.
A muddy field in western Pennsylvania. Mist rising off the still being smashed by federal axes. The smell of mash, woodsmoke, fear-sweat under flannel. A militia drummer too young to shave, keeping a beat for men older than his father.
CONRAD (looking at a farmer who will not meet his eye): "I thought I was your country. It turns out I am your landlord. I will have to learn the difference before you teach it to me the hard way."
✦ View Comic #008 — Drama Regen
▣ Storyboard in Boords ↗
✦ Drama Regen · intro card + 9-panel composite · click to expand
#009 · 1798 The Alien and Sedition Acts ✦ Jenova
Conrad Powers, Aurora Lux
Congress makes it illegal to criticize the government, afraid of French influence. Conrad (the Dollar) has his first panic attack — looking at the people inside him (the Irish, the French, the printers, the dissenters) and not recognizing his own body. Fear has disguised itself as governance. Aurora (Gold) watches from Paris and names the pattern that will repeat for centuries.
Congress passes laws restricting immigration and criminalizing criticism of the federal government, fearing French influence.
Conrad has his first panic attack. He looks around at the people inside him — the French, the Irish, the printers, the dissenters — and he does not recognize his own body. He passes the laws not out of strength but out of the kind of fear that pretends to be strength. Aurora notes that fear has a particular smell, even in a young currency.
A senate chamber in Philadelphia. Oil lamps smoking. The smell of damp wool, ink, the sour-milk note of anxiety. A draft from a window that will not close properly. Outside, a printer being arrested on his own front step.
AURORA (in a letter, never sent, to Charlotte): "He is afraid of the people inside him. Always note when a country becomes afraid of its own organs. It tells you what year it will eat itself."
✦ View Comic #009 — Drama Regen
▣ Storyboard in Boords ↗
✦ Drama Regen · intro card + 9-panel composite · click to expand
#010 · 1800 The First Transfer of Power ✦ Jenova
Conrad Powers, Charlotte Windsor
John Adams hands the presidency to his political enemy Thomas Jefferson and walks out before sunrise — no army, no funeral, no body on the floor. Charlotte (Sterling), who has watched four centuries of crowns require corpses to change heads, receives the dispatch and goes still. For the first time, she wonders if Conrad (the Dollar) is not temporary. This is the moment she starts watching him properly.
John Adams peacefully cedes the presidency to Thomas Jefferson, his political enemy. The first such transfer in modern history.
Conrad does something no empire has done before — he hands himself, voluntarily, from one man to another, without a body falling. Charlotte, who has watched four hundred years of crowns require corpses to change heads, cannot quite believe it. For the first time, she wonders if Conrad is not temporary.
A drafty room in the half-finished President's House. Plaster still drying on the walls. The smell of wet lime, cold ash in the grate, a single cup of weak coffee gone tepid. Adams putting on his hat by candlelight at four in the morning, before anyone can watch him leave.
CHARLOTTE (reading the dispatch in London, very still): "No one died. He simply walked out. I think — I think I may have miscounted him."
✦ View Comic #010 — Drama Regen
▣ Storyboard in Boords ↗
✦ Drama Regen · intro card + 9-panel composite · click to expand
#011 · 1804 The Duel ✦ Jenova
Conrad Powers
Aaron Burr shoots Alexander Hamilton at dawn on a grass ledge above the Hudson River. Hamilton was the architect of Conrad's (the Dollar's) entire financial self — the credit system, the funded debt, the First Bank. The man who built Conrad's spine is killed by the man who first taught Conrad to want power. Conrad survives them both. The survival feels like theft.
Aaron Burr, Vice President, kills Alexander Hamilton in a duel at Weehawken, New Jersey.
The man who wrote Conrad's architecture is shot dead by the man who first taught Conrad to want power. Conrad survives them both, and the survival feels like theft. He will spend two centuries pretending he chose Hamilton's vision. He didn't. He inherited it from a corpse.
A grass ledge above the Hudson at dawn. Mist on the water like a held breath. The smell of river salt and gunpowder. Two pairs of polished boots making prints in the dew. A surgeon waiting at a respectful distance with his back turned.
CONRAD (years later, alone in the Treasury, to a portrait of Hamilton): "You gave me a spine. He gave me a hunger. I have spent every day since trying to forgive myself for needing both of you."
✦ View Comic #011 — Drama Regen
▣ Storyboard in Boords ↗
✦ Drama Regen · intro card + 9-panel composite · click to expand
#012 · 1807 The Embargo ✦ Jenova
Conrad Powers, Charlotte Windsor, Aurora Lux
Jefferson closes every American port to punish Britain and France for harassing US ships. Charlotte (Sterling) barely notices — she has other suppliers. The American harbors starve instead: dockworkers gone home, chandlers shuttered, ropes mildewing on empty wharves. Aurora (Gold) delivers the diagnosis to Conrad (the Dollar) in one sentence. You cannot wound someone by withholding what they've already arranged to live without.
Jefferson's Embargo Act halts all American international trade, intending to punish Britain and France. It devastates American ports instead.
Conrad tries to wound Europe by refusing to feed it. He learns that withholding only works if the other person was hungry. Charlotte was not hungry. Conrad starves his own harbors. It is his first lesson in the geometry of leverage: you can only deny what someone wanted in the first place.
A New England dock at low tide. Ships rotting at anchor in a row, sails furled, hulls greening below the waterline. The smell of tar, dead fish, rope mildewing in coils. A dockmaster's ledger blank for the third month running.
AURORA (to Conrad, who has come to her late at night, ashamed): "Darling. You cannot punish a woman by leaving a room she had already decided to leave."
✦ View Comic #012 — Drama Regen
▣ Storyboard in Boords ↗
✦ Drama Regen · intro card + 9-panel composite · click to expand
#013 · 1814 The Burning of Washington ✦ Jenova
Conrad Powers, Charlotte Windsor
British troops burn the White House, the Capitol, and the Navy Yard on August 24, 1814. Charlotte (Sterling) rides out to watch from a hill east of the city — not in triumph, but in housekeeping. She expected this experiment to fail. Conrad (the Dollar) stands beside her, watching his own house burn in three colors, and makes a decision that will define the next 250 years: destruction is not defeat, unless you let it be.
British troops burn the White House, the Capitol, and most public buildings in Washington, D.C.
Charlotte walks into Conrad's house and sets it on fire — not in rage, but in housekeeping. She is tidying up an experiment she still believes will be canceled. Conrad watches the smoke from a hill outside the city and understands, with a clarity that will define the next century, that survival is not the same as being respected. He will have to become something Charlotte cannot tidy.
A hill east of Washington at twilight. The capital below burning in three places — the President's House, the Capitol, the Navy Yard — each fire a different color against the August sky. The smell of woodsmoke, paper ash, river damp. A horse pawing the ground because it cannot understand why no one is moving.
CONRAD (watching the fires reflect in his own eyes, very quiet): "Burn it. Burn all of it. You have just taught me that I am the only one who can decide whether I exist tomorrow."
✦ View Comic #013 — Drama Regen
▣ Storyboard in Boords ↗
✦ Drama Regen · intro card + 9-panel composite · click to expand
Era 2 — Industrial Ascent
1815–1865
17 moments
#014 · 1848 Gold Changes Everything
Conrad Powers, Aurora Lux
James Marshall finds gold at Sutter's Mill in California. Within a year, 300,000 people are moving west.
Aurora is no longer a rumor from Europe. She is in the dirt of Conrad's own body. He cannot pretend to be indifferent to her anymore — she has been found inside him. For the first time, he wants her not as a backing or a metaphor but as a woman. The seduction has reversed direction. He is the one who arrived late.
A creek bed in the Sierra foothills at noon. Cold water over yellow stone. The smell of pine sap, sweat, gunpowder from a hunter's rifle two ridges over. Aurora stands ankle-deep in the stream, her hair so bright the prospectors think it is a trick of the sun. It is not.
AURORA (to Conrad, who has ridden three weeks to find her): "You spent fifty years pretending you didn't need me. Now you've dug up half a mountain to ask. Sit down. We have a great deal to negotiate."
#015 · 1817 The Erie Canal
Conrad Powers
New York begins construction of the Erie Canal, eventually connecting the Great Lakes to the Atlantic via the Hudson.
Conrad, for the first time, reaches inside himself and rearranges his own organs. Buffalo can now speak to New York without asking Europe for permission. The internal market is born — and with it, the dangerous American conviction that any obstacle is a thing to be dug through rather than walked around.
A muddy trench in upstate New York. Irish laborers waist-deep in clay, singing in a language the foreman does not understand. The smell of wet earth, lamp oil, blackpowder smoke. A young engineer's drawings staked into the bank, the corners curling in the damp.
CONRAD (to the engineer, who has just asked if it is truly possible): "Anything is possible if you are willing to cut a river out of a country with a spoon. We are. That is the only sentence I need you to remember about me."
#016 · 1820 The Missouri Compromise
Conrad Powers, Aurora Lux
Congress admits Missouri as a slave state and Maine as a free state, drawing a line at the 36°30' parallel to defer the question of slavery's expansion.
Conrad postpones the conversation that will, in forty years, tear him in half. He calls it wisdom. Aurora calls it interest, compounding. She has watched men defer truth before. The interest rate on a deferred truth is always the same — it doubles every generation until the bill arrives in blood.
A House cloakroom. Cigar smoke trapped against a low ceiling. The smell of bourbon, hair pomade, the brass tang of a spittoon used too often. A map of the new territories spread on a card table, a pencil line drawn straight across the West.
AURORA (alone, after the vote, running her finger along the pencil line): "A boundary on a map is a promise. A promise this thin is a debt. He will pay it. With interest. With sons."
#017 · 1825 The First Railroad
Conrad Powers
The Baltimore & Ohio Railroad is chartered. Within a decade, rail will outpace canal, road, river — every prior form of American distance.
Conrad begins to move faster than the people inside him. A man in Ohio learns of his father's death in Maryland in two days instead of three weeks. Grief, news, panic, rumor — all now travel at the same speed. Conrad does not yet understand that compressing time is the same as compressing tempers.
A boiler shop in Baltimore. Iron filings glittering on the floor like a black snow. The smell of coal, hot grease, burnt cotton waste. A locomotive half-assembled, ribbed like a whale's skeleton. A foreman shouting over the hiss of a test boiler.
CONRAD (running a hand along the cold iron flank of the engine): "I have just bought myself a body that can outrun my own conscience. God help whoever stands between the two."
#018 · 1837 The Panic of 1837
Conrad Powers, Charlotte Ashworth
Land speculation and the collapse of state banks trigger a five-year depression. Banks suspend specie payment.
Conrad's first public humiliation as an adult. He is found, in the noon light, to have promised more than he held. Charlotte reads about it over breakfast in London and allows herself, briefly, the cruelty of a small smile. Then she remembers that her own debtors are also his debtors, and her tea goes cold.
A Wall Street countinghouse the morning of the suspension. A line of men around the block. The smell of wet wool, panic-sweat, oil from the lamps still burning at midmorning because no one thought to put them out. A clerk weeping behind a brass grille.
CHARLOTTE (folding the newspaper carefully, to her husband across the breakfast table): "He has failed publicly. Good. Let him learn that the difference between a country and a confidence trick is one bad morning."
#019 · 1857 The Panic
Conrad Powers, Aurora Lux
The collapse of the Ohio Life Insurance and Trust Company triggers a worldwide financial panic — the first transmitted instantly by telegraph.
Conrad's humiliation is no longer a local matter. The telegraph has made his shame global, simultaneous, unedited. Aurora watches him stagger and feels, for the first time, something close to tenderness. He is large enough now to be hurt in public. It is the first adult quality she has seen in him.
